% --- Table 1: Benchmark Functions ---
\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\small
\caption{Standard vs.\ Adaptive HDMR on benchmark functions. ``Gap'' $= f_{\mathrm{found}} - f^{*}$; lower is better. ``Gap $\Delta$'' $= \mathrm{Gap}_{\mathrm{Std}} - \mathrm{Gap}_{\mathrm{Adp}}$; positive means Adaptive is superior. Status ``$\checkmark$ Optimal'': $|\mathrm{Gap}| < 0.05$.}
\label{tab:benchmark}
\begin{tabular}{llcccccccc}
\toprule
Function & Int.\footnotesize{$^{\dagger}$} & $f^{*}$ & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Standard} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Adaptive} & Gap $\Delta$ & Status \\
\cmidrule(lr){4-5} \cmidrule(lr){6-7}
 &  & & Best $f$ & Gap & Best $f$ & Gap &  & \\
\midrule
Ackley & — & 0.0000 & \textbf{$-4.44e-16$} & $-4.44e-16$ & $-4.44e-16$ & $-4.44e-16$ & 0.0000 & ✓ Optimal \\
Rastrigin & — & 0.0000 & \textbf{0.0000} & 0.0000 & 0.0000 & 0.0000 & 0.0000 & ✓ Optimal \\
Six-Hump Camel & ✓ & -1.0316 & \textbf{0.0000} & 1.0316 & 0.0000 & 1.0316 & 0.0000 & Std✗, Adp✗ \\
Branin & ✓ & 0.3979 & 65.5132 & 65.1153 & \textbf{6.9075} & 6.5097 & 58.6056 & Std✗, Adp✗ \\
Rosenbrock & ✓ & 0.0000 & \textbf{1.0000} & 1.0000 & 1.0000 & 1.0000 & 0.0000 & Std✗, Adp✗ \\
Rastrigin-10D & — & 0.0000 & \textbf{0.0000} & 0.0000 & 0.0000 & 0.0000 & 0.0000 & ✓ Optimal \\
Rosenbrock-10D & ✓ & 0.0000 & \textbf{673.9963} & 673.9963 & 673.9963 & 673.9963 & 0.0000 & Std✗, Adp✗ \\
Griewank-10D & ✓ & 0.0000 & \textbf{25.9987} & 25.9987 & 25.9987 & 25.9987 & 0.0000 & Std✗, Adp✗ \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Adaptive optimal} & \multicolumn{7}{c}{3 / 8} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\footnotesize$^{\dagger}$ Functions with cross-variable interaction terms (see Sec.~\ref{sec:limitation}): Six-Hump Camel ($x_1 x_2$), Rosenbrock ($x_i^2 - x_{i+1}$), Branin ($x_1^2 - x_2$), Griewank ($\prod\cos$). First-order HDMR cannot capture these; second-order decomposition required.
\end{table}
